title: [Feature]: Evaluate optional InProgress handling for long sink writes
problem: Long-running sink writes may exceed consumer ACK wait settings if the destination is slow but still making progress. JetStream supports progress-style signals, but adding them carelessly could hide stalled writes or make delivery semantics harder to reason about.
proposal: Evaluate and possibly implement optional InProgress handling for long-running sink writes. The design should use bounded intervals, clear shutdown behavior, metrics, and tests proving that progress signals never replace durable success or final ACK.
users: Operators with large batches or slow destinations, maintainers tuning backpressure and redelivery behavior, and reviewers assessing long-running transaction safety.
delivery_semantics: InProgress signals, if implemented, must be advisory only. Final ACK still happens only after durable sink success. If a sink fails after progress signals, the message must remain eligible for redelivery or DLQ according to policy.
security: Progress logs and metrics must not include payloads, message identifiers, or sensitive metadata by default. Intervals and counts must be bounded to avoid noisy or unbounded behavior.
acceptance: The design documents when InProgress is appropriate and when it is unsafe.
Configuration is optional, bounded, and disabled by default.
Tests prove progress signals do not ACK messages.
tests: Add mocked timing tests for progress intervals, sink success, sink failure, shutdown, and retry. Run scripts/check.sh.
documentation: Update docs/commit-then-ack.md, docs/configuration.md, docs/operations.md, docs/metrics.md, ROADMAP.md, and CHANGELOG.md.
closeout: Close after implemented release or documented no-go decision. Include release tag or decision evidence, tests, and operational caveats.
